title: Tangy Crunchy Hail Stir-fry
description: A tangy and indulgent Korean dish featuring hails (a type of seaweed) that are blanched before being stir-fried with other crunchy vegetables for a delightful, kosher-friendly meal.
instructions: Wash and slice the hails. Blanch them in boiling water for about 3 minutes, then drain.
In a small bowl, mix soy sauce, rice vinegar, and sugar until the sugar is dissolved.
Heat a wok or large skillet over medium-high heat. Add oil, then add the blanched hails and bell peppers. Stir-fry for 2-3 minutes.
Add scallions and red pepper, stir-frying for another minute until vegetables are just tender.
Pour the soy sauce mixture into the wok or skillet, stirring to coat everything evenly. Add red pepper flakes if using, then toss everything together briefly.
Serve hot as a side dish with rice.
